---
title: "MGT 816 — The Private Firm CFO"
---

## Accounting and Finance Strategies

This course equips students with the core set of tools and strategies related to accounting and financial reporting from the perspective of a Chief Financial Officer at a private, entrepreneurial company. The class focuses on accounting-based financial metrics, financial reporting, and managerial accounting, with an emphasis on structuring the accounting system from the start-up phase through to exit.

The course follows the life of the company — from financial infrastructure through capitalization, equity compensation, forecasting, board reporting, revenue recognition, fundraising, and exits. Two cases anchor the term: a Harvard Business School case on a serial founding team, and an original case on the tax consequences of employee stock options.

### What you will take from the course

Public-company finance is well covered elsewhere in the curriculum. The private firm is different: there is no market price, no analyst following, and little mandated disclosure, and the owners — founders, employees, and funds — hold claims with different rights and different incentives. The CFO sits where those claims meet.

By the end of the term you will have built the working documents of that seat yourself: a capitalization table constructed from formation through a priced Series A; a driver-based operating forecast, built with AI tools doing the analyst work and you certifying every number; a board memo on equity compensation and its tax consequences; and a critique of a board reporting package.

::: {.hero-buttons}
[Schedule](schedule.qmd)
[Syllabus PDF](files/syllabus.pdf)
[Canvas](CANVAS_URL_PLACEHOLDER){target="_blank"}
[Materials](materials.qmd)
:::

### This week

::: {.this-week}
**Week of September 2.** First class Wednesday, September 2 — Case 1 (FeedBurner) assigned; memo due Wednesday, September 16 at 1:00 pm.

**Coming up:** CFO Toolkit Bootcamp Friday, September 4 (required Monday-makeup class).
:::

*Update the "This week" block above after each class day (2 lines).*
